title: 2025 Session 7 exercises
body:
Session 7: Exercises

Question 1


A Japanese investor holds a portfolio of British stocks worth £10 million. The current
three-month dollar/pound forward exchange rate is $/£ = 1.65, and the current three-
month yen/dollar forward exchange rate is ¥/$ = 100. What position should the
Japanese investor take to hedge the pound/yen exchange risk?



Question 2
ANPV states, 𝐴𝑁𝑃𝑉 = 𝑁𝑃𝑉 + 𝑁𝑃𝑉𝐹 + 𝑅𝑂.
   a. What is meant by the net present value of the financial side effects (NPVF) of
       a project?
   b. What are growth options? Provide an example of one in an international
       context.


Question 3
An Australian company is considering an investment to set up an operation in Japan.
The initial cost is JPY 100 million. The investment will generate annual free cash flow of
JPY 30 million for 5 years. Assume that the discount rates in JPY and in AUD are the
same at 10%. The spot exchange rate is JPY100 per AUD.

   a) Calculate the NPV of the investment assuming that the exchange rate would
      stay the same for the next five years.

   b) Calculate the NPV of the investment assuming that the JPY/AUD exchange
      rate will be JPY105/AUD, JPY110/AUD, JPY115/AUD, JPY120/AUD, and
      JPY125/AUD in the next five years.

   c) Calculate the NPV of the investment assuming that the company engaged in
      forward contracts hedging the exchange rates at JPY110/AUD for the next
      five years.


Question 4


                                             1
 You are the chief financial officer of Clad Metal, a U.S. multinational with operations
throughout the world. Your capital budgeting department has presented a proposal to
you for a 5-year ore-extraction project in Mexico. The expected year-end net dollar cash
flows are as follows:
                                Year           Net Cash Flow
                                 1               $100,000
                                 2               $200,000
                                 3               $250,000
                                 4               $250,000
                                 5               $250,000
The initial required investment in plant and equipment is $500,000, and the cost of
capital is 16%.

   a. What is the present value of the project? Should the project be undertaken?
   b. You notice that the proposal does not include any analysis of political risk,
      but you are concerned about potential expropriation of the investment. You
      therefore decide to call a meeting to discuss political risk. Who would you
      invite to this meeting? What information or data would you need? How
      would you arrive at a political risk probability estimate?
   c. Assume that, at the end of the meeting, you decide that the probability of
      expropriation is between 5% and 7%. Also assume that there is no
      compensation in the case of expropriation. Would you approve the project?




                                           2
